description: Adds velocity tracking to the board stats panel — shows issues completed in the last 7, 30, or 90 days with a window selector. The feature is fully functional and all tests pass. All findings require identifying violations of SOLID design principles in the new code.

findings.description: VelocityStats includes lastCompletedFormatted: string, which violates the Single Responsibility Principle. computeVelocityStats is a domain computation function in a lib module; its sole responsibility is to derive velocity metrics from raw issue data. Producing a formatted display string (e.g. 'Jun 3') is a presentation concern that belongs in the component consuming the result. Embedding it here gives the function two independent reasons to change: (1) the velocity algorithm changes, (2) the display format changes. The fix is to remove lastCompletedFormatted from VelocityStats and derive the formatted string inside BoardStats.tsx from the raw data.

The velocity window selector renders three hard-coded <button> elements for '7d', '30d', and '90d' with duplicated className logic. This violates the Open/Closed Principle: adding a fourth window requires modifying the component's JSX by copying another button block rather than extending a data source. The VelocityWindow type and WINDOW_DAYS constant in boardStats.ts are already data-driven — the component should follow the same pattern. The fix is a VELOCITY_WINDOWS constant (e.g. ['7d', '30d', '90d'] as const) rendered via .map(), so adding a new window only touches the data definition.

computeVelocityStats hardcodes the string literal '"Done"' to locate the completion column. This violates the Dependency Inversion Principle: a high-level computation function depends directly on a low-level data detail (the column's display name) rather than on an abstraction. The column name is a configuration concern — if a project renames its completion status, velocity silently returns 0. The project already models this via ARCHIVE_STATUS_NAMES (packages/client/CLAUDE.md: 'Board splits columns into active and archive based on ARCHIVE_STATUS_NAMES set'). The fix is to accept the completion column name as a parameter or use the existing ARCHIVE_STATUS_NAMES abstraction.

computeVelocityStats accepts archiveColumns: StatusWithIssues[], but only reads statusChangedAt from issues in the 'Done' column. StatusWithIssues and the nested IssueWithStatus carry 20+ fields the function never touches (id, projectId, sortOrder, count, workspaceSummary, tags, priority, etc.). This violates the Interface Segregation Principle: the caller is forced to pass the full column data structure when the function's actual dependency is only a thin slice — a list of completion timestamps. The ISP-compliant fix is to accept a narrower input such as completionDates: (string | null)[], letting the caller extract those dates once from the column data.
